headline: Production agent infrastructure dominated the week as memory systems, local inference, and observability moved to the foreground.
body: The clearest rotation is toward operationalizing AI agents: an AI Research OS turns 10,000+ Obsidian notes into token-based memory without vector or graph databases, and OpenGov detailed scaling agents across thousands of governments with evals, monitoring, and observability. On the model side, the Ornith family from DeepReinforce ships 9B to 397B agentic coding models with GGUF builds optimized for local deployment, while real-time voice work centers on streaming audio, interruption handling, and turn detection. Breakout techniques cluster around the same theme: human-in-the-loop, observability, function calling, and system prompts all surfaced as new or rising entrants. Gemini Canvas and discussion of next training paradigms covering verification and reinforcement learning round out the window.

cited_items.title: Turn 10,994 Notes Into Memory - Paul Iusztin, Decoding AI & Louis-François Bouchard, Towards AI
